terms.definition: Expected product of mean-centered variables: Cov(X, Y) = E[(X - μ_X)(Y - μ_Y)]; measures joint linear co-movement in units of X times units of Y.
ρ(X, Y) = Cov(X, Y) / (σ_X σ_Y); dimensionless linear association in [-1, 1], invariant to positive scaling of X or Y.
Unbiased estimate s_XY = (1/(n - 1)) Σ (xᵢ - x̄)(yᵢ - ȳ) from paired observations; building block of the sample covariance matrix.
Symmetric matrix Σ with Σ_ij = Cov(X_i, X_j); diagonal entries are variances, off-diagonals are pairwise covariances for a random vector.
Matrix of Pearson correlations between variables; diagonals are 1, off-diagonals in [-1, 1]; equals the covariance matrix of standardized features.
Theoretical covariance Cov(X, Y) for random variables X and Y, as opposed to a sample estimate computed from finite data.
